description:
Goal: publish the ownership cut for the compiler2-native backend before code moves. Signal: docs and contract tests state exactly which artifacts a compiler2-native backend may read after `NativeProgram(root)`, which lower-level backend/runtime mechanics remain genuinely shared, and which old-world/codegen shapes are illegal as the long-term compiler2-native contract.

Change:
- document the ownership cut in northstar and agent docs
- turn the existing native-handoff assertions in `src/compiler2/artifact_test.rs` into the explicit contract for the in-house backend move
- map the remaining old-world dependencies (`SpecPlan`, `SpecRegistry`, `crate::types::Ty`, legacy dispatch/type-test reprojection, old `ExternDecl` payloads) to either compiler2 facts that should replace them or temporary compiler2-local baggage that must later be deleted

Why:
- without a precise cut line, the fork will calcify into a permanent compatibility layer under new names

Checks:
- docs cite the real current files and artifacts
- tests/notes make illegal backend questions visible
title: Compiler2 native backends: publish the in-house native-codegen contract
